rejection_reasons: per-stone authored-zone side UVs read as clean gray blocks rather than source weathered stone
open joints expose the review background instead of recessed red/dark mortar
course stones remain too mechanically uniform in vertical cadence
C002 side patches sample source joint/background edges and produce visible stripes
C002/C003 stone silhouettes remain too rectangular and vertically uniform
C004 rubble cadence remains too even
C002 source-core side patch routing still reads as metallic vertical striping
the C004 mortar bed reaches the visible perimeter and hides the individual rubble as a smooth skirt
the recessed mortar does not bridge the two measured inter-course clearances, leaving bright background bands
C004 resolves as a uniform authored-gray block cadence rather than the source-visible rubble apron
the exposed course ledges are untextured authored gray and lose the approved top-view weathering
C004 height variation remains too uniform for the source rubble silhouette

acceptance_basis: C002 and C003 remain visibly separate individual-stone courses with recessed joints
C004 reads as 32 source-weathered irregular rubble stones without a continuous skirt
source-weathered top faces are restored and bright inter-course background bands are absent
the A08 independent technical audit passed all 17 pre-final gates
